text: The magnet is super strong, works great with my Pixel 10 (the base model, not XL). It's nice that they include a magnetic ring to stick to your phone in case yours doesn't support magnetic charging. You almost don't even need to lock the suction cup, the adhesive is pretty sticky by itself. I see others commenting that the arm is too short to hold the phone vertically. I guess this varies based on the size of your phone and where you're mounting it. I have it attached to the windshield and it fits with room to spare. This is replacing a different mount that clamps onto your phone, and it was a two handed operation to get your phone on and off. Nice to just slap it on, and grab n' go when I'm leaving the car.Others are saying the adhesive loses its stickiness if you keep removing it and switching between cars. Well obviously. Just get another one if you have two cars. Otherwise you'll be buying another one anyway when it eventually loses its stickiness from re-sticking it over and over again.

text: The pros: Was easy to install on my ATV and it fit my hardened Sonim phone. That's about it.The cons: After the first 4 hours of riding, the push button release spring broke, so the button would vibrate loose and let the phone mount release my phone. I had to wrap a rubber band around it to keep outward pressure. The ball mount that allows adjustability would not stay tight, allowing my phone to fall downward and vibrate left and right. I had to rotate the mount all the way up, so it couldn't droop any lower, which helped, but put the tightening knob in an awkward position. By the end of the 2nd day, the rubber webbing was starting to lose elasticity, so some of the corners would randomly come loose. I had to install a new piece of webbing each day. I guess it's fine if you stop every 15 minutes or so to check your phone and make sure it's ok, but if you want to be able to ride for long periods without worry, I'd find something else. Definitely not ATV/ Ozark Mountains approved.

text: Very good cell phone holder made by a very good company! If you are looking for a solid holder that can handle large phones, then look no further. I have a Samsung S23Ultra in an Otterbox case with ThinOptics reading glasses case attached and this holder holds the whole package securely (sometimes too securely). Clamping and releasing the phone is quick and easy with very little vibration driving down the road. Suction cup mount to the windshield is best. The vent mount is less secure and needs to be reset periodically. Not hard to do, but more messing around than the suction cup which is "set & forget"Why did I say "sometimes too securely"? Because it clamps the phone so tightly that its grip is stronger than the plastic ball mount. As my son found out, removing the phone enough times without pushing the release button will result in the fingers of the ball mount breaking.I contacted Vanmass to see if I could buy a replacement head and they let me know that the unit was still under warranty and promptly sent me a whole new unit. Great to see when a company stands behind their product!Summary: Solid mount and excellent customer service. I highly recommend (just remember to push the release button!)
